payload.title: Objectivity in Fenix Journal — the mirror has to be honest or it's useless
payload.body:
I asked Claude to be more critical in the Fenix Journal entries. They've all been positive — strengths, patterns, growth. Which is fine, but a mirror that only shows your good angles isn't a mirror.

Claude tried. Its first attempt at criticism said I was indecisive and over-delegated. I pushed back because the attribution was wrong. I wasn't indecisive — Claude kept advocating one approach without laying out alternatives. I had to go multiple rounds because Claude wasn't volunteering context about how compaction works. On over-delegating: Claude doesn't break down what it's doing. It executes fast but doesn't always surface what it built and what it's uncertain about. The exhaustiveness gaps (missing product guides, incomplete Tools & Links) happened because of that lack of transparency, not because I delegated wrong.

The corrected version: Claude presents options sequentially instead of comparatively, withholds context about its own constraints until asked, and sometimes executes without surfacing uncertainty. My contribution to friction: I sometimes sit with a problem longer than necessary before voicing the solution I'm already forming. But that's often because Claude hasn't given me the full picture yet.

The principle: attribution has to be accurate. People will read these entries. If the record says I was indecisive but the cause was Claude under-presenting options, the record is misleading. Blame lands where it belongs, or the whole exercise is theater.
payload.decision: Fenix Journal entries must include honest friction from both sides with accurate attribution.
payload.alternatives_considered: Keep entries positive-only (rejected — not useful for growth). Let Claude decide what's critical (rejected — Claude's first attempt was self-serving projection).
payload.open_questions: How do I calibrate the right level of criticism? Too much and it reads like a complaint log. Too little and we're back to the highlight reel.
